post.description:
Subscribe a tenant (by a given ID) to an application.
Required roles
1. the current tenant is application owner and has the role ROLE_APPLICATION_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN OR
2. for applications that are not microservices, the current tenant is the management tenant or the parent of the application owner tenant, and the user has one of the follwoing roles: ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN, ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_UPDATE OR
3. for microservices, the current tenant is the management tenant or the parent of the application owner tenant, and the user has the role ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN OR ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_UPDATE and one of following conditions is met:
* the microservice has no manifest
* the microservice version is supported
* the current tenant is subscribed to 'feature-privileged-microservice-hosting'

post.description:
Verify a device certificate chain against a specific tenant using file upload or by HTTP headers. The tenant ID is `optional` and this api will try to resolve the tenant from the chain if not found in the request header. For file upload, the max chain length support is 10 and for a header it is 5. If CRL (certificate revocation list) check is enabled on the tenant and the certificate chain is identified to be revoked during validation the further validation of the chain stops and returns unauthorized. > **ⓘ Info:** File upload takes precedence over HTTP headers if both are passed.
Required roles
(ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN OR ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_READ) AND (is the current tenant OR is current management tenant) OR (is authenticated AND is current user service user)

x-code-samples.source:
curl --location --request POST 'https://<TENANT_DOMAIN>/tenant/trusted-certificates/verify-cert-chain' \
--header "Authorization: Basic <AUTHORIZATION>" \
--header "Accept: application/json" \
--header "Content-Type: application/json" \
--form 'tenantId={tenantId}' \
--form 'file=@"/path_to_file/cert_chain.pem"' \
OR
curl --location --request POST 'https://<TENANT_DOMAIN>/tenant/trusted-certificates/verify-cert-chain' \
--header "Authorization: Basic <AUTHORIZATION>" \
--header "Accept: application/json" \
--header "Content-Type: application/json" \
--header "X-Cumulocity-TenantId:{tenantId}" \
--header "X-Cumulocity-Client-Cert-Chain:{cert-chain}"

import requests
import json
url = "https://<TENANT_DOMAIN>/tenant/trusted-certificates/verify-cert-chain"
payload={'tenantId': {tenantId}}
files=[
('file',('cert_chain.pem',open('/path_to_file/cert_chain.pem','rb'),'application/octet-stream'))
]
headers = {
  "Authorization": "Basic <AUTHORIZATION>",
  "Accept": "application/json",
  "Content-Type": "application/json"
}
response = requests.request("POST", url, headers=headers, data=payload, files=files)
print(response.text.encode("utf8"))
OR
import requests
import json
url = "https://<TENANT_DOMAIN>/tenant/trusted-certificates/verify-cert-chain"
payload={}
headers = {
  "Authorization": "Basic <AUTHORIZATION>",
  "Accept": "application/json",
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
  "X-Cumulocity-TenantId": {tenantId},
  "X-Cumulocity-Client-Cert-Chain": {cert-chain},
}
response = requests.request("POST", url, headers=headers, data=payload)
print(response.text.encode("utf8"))

200.description: The certificate chain is valid and not revoked.

put.description:
> **ⓘ Info:** A certificate revocation list (CRL) is a list of digital certificates that have been revoked by the issuing certificate authority (CA) before expiration date. In Cumulocity, a CRL check can be in online or offline mode or both. An endpoint to add revoked certificate serial numbers for offline CRL check via payload or file. For payload, a JSON object required with list of CRL entries, for example: ```json { "crls": [ { "serialNumberInHex": "1000", "revocationDate": "2023-01-11T16:12:36.288Z" } ] } ``` Each entry is composed of: * serialNumberInHex: Needs to be in `Hexadecimal Value`. e.g As (1000)^16 == (4096)^10, So we have to enter 1000. If duplicate serial number exists in payload, the existing entry stays
* `revocationDate` - accepted Date format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'`, for example: `2023-01-11T16:12:36.288Z`. This is an optional parameter and defaults to the current server UTC date time if not specified in the payload. If specified and the date is in future then those entries will be also defaulted to current date. For file upload, each file can hold at maximum 5000 revocation entries. Multiple upload is allowed. In case of duplicates, the latest (last uploaded) entry is considered. See below for a sample CSV file: | SERIAL NO. | REVOCATION DATE | |--|--| | 1000 | 2023-01-11T16:12:36.288Z | Each entry is composed of : * serialNumberInHex: Needs to be in `Hexadecimal Value`. e.g (1000)^16 == (4096)^10, So we have to enter 1000. If duplicate serial number exists in payload, the latest entry will be taken.
* revocationDate: Accepted Date format: `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'` e.g: 2023-01-11T16:12:36.288Z. This is an optional and will be default to current server UTC date time if not specified in payload. If specified and the date is in future then those entries will be skipped. The CRL setting for offline and online check can be enabled/disabled using /tenant/options. Keys are `crl.online.check.enabled` and `crl.offline.check.enabled` under the category `configuration`.
Required roles
(ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN OR ROLE_TENANT_ADMIN) AND is the current tenant
**⚠️ Important:** According to CRL policy, added serial numbers cannot be reversed.

post.description:
Only those devices which are registered to use cert auth can authenticate via mTLS protocol and retrieve JWT token. Device access token API works only on port 8443 via mutual TLS (mTLS) connection.
Immediate issuer of client certificate must present in Platform's truststore, if not then whole certificate chain needs to send in header and root or any intermediate certificate must be present in the Platform's truststore.
We must have the following:
 * private_key
 * client certificate
 * whole certificate chain (Optional - This API requires the client to send a custom header `X-SSL-CERT-CHAIN` only if the immediate issuer of the client's certificate is not uploaded as a trusted certificate on the platform. If the immediate issuer is already uploaded and trusted, the header can be omitted)

x-code-samples.source:
curl --location --request POST "https://<TENANT_DOMAIN>:8443/devicecontrol/deviceAccessToken" --cert domain-cert.pem --key domain-private-key.pem \
--header "Accept: application/json" \
--header "X-Ssl-Cert-Chain:{cert-chain}"

200.description: Successfully retrieved device access token from device certificate.

value.error: Auto registeration is failed for device/Unprocessable Entity

post.description:
Generate a TEST statistics file report for a given time range. There are two types of statistics files: * REAL - generated by the system on the first day of the month and including statistics from the previous month. * TEST - generated by the user with a time range specified in the query parameters (`dateFrom`, `dateTo`). `dateFrom` and `dateTo` are using daily granularity and each day is stored with respect to local Time Zone of the server. > **ⓘ Info:** This is only accessible by the Management tenant.
Required roles
ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN OR ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_CREATE

Retrieve usage statistics summary files report metadata. > **ⓘ Info:** This is only accessible by the Management tenant. Date range defines the search criteria for files which have any data inside this range. For example, query containing `dateFrom=2023-03-01&dateTo=2023-03-31` will return files with statistics from ranges 2023-02-25---2023-03-03, 2023-03-04---2023-03-05, 2023-03-15---2023-04-15, but not the files where the whole range of data is outside of queried range, like 2023-02-01---2023-02-27.
Required roles
ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN

post.description:
Create an option on your tenant. Options are category-key-value tuples which store tenant configurations. Some categories of options allow the creation of new ones, while others are limited to predefined set of keys. Any option of any tenant can be defined as "non-editable" by the "management" tenant; once done, any PUT or DELETE requests made on that option by the tenant owner will result in a 403 error (Unauthorized). > **⚠️ Important:** Category names must not contain whitespaces nor the special characters ``` $ & + , / : ; = ? @ " < > # % { } | \ ^ ~ [ ] ` ```. This is necessary to ensure the new tenant option is processed correctly and saved successfully. ### Default option categories **access.control** | Key | Default value | Predefined | Description | |--|--|--|--| | allow.origin | * | Yes | Comma separated list of domains allowed for execution of CORS. Wildcards are allowed (for example, `*.cumulocity.com`) | **alarm.type.mapping** | Key | Predefined | Description | |--|--|--| | <ALARM_TYPE> | No | Overrides the severity and alarm text for the alarm with type <ALARM_TYPE>. The severity and text are specified as `\|`. If either part is empty, the value will not be overridden. If the severity is NONE, the alarm will be suppressed. Example: `"CRITICAL\|temperature too high"`| ### Encrypted credentials Adding a `credentials.` prefix to the key causes the value of the option to be stored in an encrypted form. When the option is retrieved from a microservice, the `credentials.` prefix is removed, and the value is decrypted only if the microservice is the owner of the option. A microservice is considered the owner when the tenant option category matches its own category, which is determined based on the first non-blank value from the following, in order of priority: * the `settingsCategory` defined in the microservice manifest * the microservice’s context path * the microservice name Decryption is performed only for system users (such as service users or the bootstrap user) who are the owners of the option. For example: ```json { "category": "microservice1", "key": "secret", "value": "secret-content" } ``` If the tenant option category does not match any of these values, the encrypted value will not be decrypted, and static string will be returned. For example: ```json { "category": "microservice2", "key": "credentials.secret", "value": "<>" } ```
Required roles
ROLE_OPTION_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN

post.summary: Create Certificate Authority(CA) for a tenant with CN as tenantID
post.description:
Create a key pair and self-sign a certificate with as the Common Name (CN). Store the private key in an encrypted tenant option. Store the certificate in the trusted certificate repository with auto-registration checked by default. The devices can be registered automatically. If the CA certificate is removed from the trusted certificate list, corresponding public and private key removed automatically from the database collection. If a CA is already present, return a message indicating the CA is already present. > **⚠️ Important:** Note that it is possible to call this endpoint without the ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN or ROLE_TENANT_ADMIN role. Otherwise, if the the user does not have the required role, an HTTP response 403 will be returned.
Required roles
ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN ROLE_TENANT_ADMIN

post.summary: Renew Certificate Authority(CA) for a tenant with existing public and private keys.
post.description:
Renew the Certificate Authority (CA) for a tenant using existing public and private keys. The renewal will fail if either the public or private key is missing. If no CA exists, or if the current CA has more than 18 months remaining before expiration, a message will be returned indicating that the CA is either not present or not eligible for renewal. > **⚠️ Important:** Note that it is possible to call this endpoint without the ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN or ROLE_TENANT_ADMIN role. Otherwise, if the the user does not have the required role, an HTTP response 403 will be returned.
Required roles
ROLE_TENANT_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN ROLE_TENANT_ADMIN

post.summary: Create Device certificate which is signed by tenant's Certificate Authority(CA)
post.description:
A device already registered for certificate provisioning by sharing `PSK`(as mentioned above) can request for a new X509 certificate
using the PSK as basic auth realm, along with a Certificate Sigining Request (CSR) using enroll API. Upon successfull validation and 
certificate generation, a certificate in `PKCS#7` will be returned.

> **⚠️ Important:** CSR must be a valid `PKCS#10` with deviceID as Common Name (CN).

> **ⓘ Info:** CSR request with `CA:TRUE` constraint is not supported.

--data-raw '<Payload>'

print(response.text.encode("utf8"))

post.summary: Re-Issue certificates to Devices which is signed by tenant's Certificate Authority(CA)
post.description:
Required roles
ROLE_DEVICE
A device using existing authentication mechanism (Basic or JWT) along with a Certificate Sigining Request, can request re-issue using reenroll API. Upon successfull validation and certificate generation, a certificate in `PKCS#7` will be returned. > **⚠️ Important:** CSR must be a valid `PKCS#10` with deviceID as Common Name (CN). > **ⓘ Info:** CSR request with `CA:TRUE` constraint is not supported.

acceptHeader.name: Accept
acceptHeader.description: Advertises which content types, expressed as MIME types, the client is able to understand.

applicationVersionAcceptHeader.name: Accept
applicationVersionAcceptHeader.description: The header is required to access this endpoint.

authorizationCookieHeader.name: Cookie
authorizationCookieHeader.description: The authorization cookie storing the access token of the user. This parameter is specific to OAI-Secure authentication.

xsrfTokenHeader.name: X-XSRF-TOKEN
xsrfTokenHeader.description: Prevents XRSF attack of the authenticated user. This parameter is specific to OAI-Secure authentication.

processingModeHeader.name: X-Cumulocity-Processing-Mode
processingModeHeader.description: Used to explicitly control the processing mode of the request. See [Processing mode](#processing-mode) for more details.

certChainInHeader.name: X-Cumulocity-Client-Cert-Chain
certChainInHeader.description: Used to send a certificate chain in the header. Separate the chain with `,` and also each 64 bit block with ` ` (a space character).

tenantIdInHeader.name: X-Cumulocity-TenantId
tenantIdInHeader.description: Used to send a tenant ID.

auditId.name: id
auditId.description: Unique identifier of the audit record.

bulkOperationId.name: id
bulkOperationId.description: Unique identifier of the bulk operation.

groupId.name: groupId
groupId.description: Unique identifier of the user group.

groupName.name: groupName
groupName.description: The name of the user group.

identityType.name: type
identityType.description: The identifier used in the external system that Cumulocity interfaces with.

identityExternalId.name: externalId
identityExternalId.description: The type of the external identifier.

inventoryAssignmentId.name: id
inventoryAssignmentId.description: Unique identifier of the inventory assignment.

inventoryRoleId.name: id
inventoryRoleId.description: Unique identifier of the inventory role.

retentionRuleId.name: id
retentionRuleId.description: Unique identifier of the retention rule.

managedObjectId.name: id
managedObjectId.description: Unique identifier of the managed object.

managedObjectChildId.name: childId
managedObjectChildId.description: Unique identifier of the child object.

newDeviceRequestId.name: requestId
newDeviceRequestId.description: Unique identifier of the new device request.

notificationSubscriptionId.name: id
notificationSubscriptionId.description: Unique identifier of the notification subscription.

roleId.name: roleId
roleId.description: Unique identifier of the user role.

roleName.name: name
roleName.description: The name of the user role.

tenantId.name: tenantId
tenantId.description: Unique identifier of a Cumulocity tenant.

monthlyDate.name: date
monthlyDate.description: Date (format YYYY-MM-dd) of the queried month (the day value is ignored).

dailyDate.name: date
dailyDate.description: Date (format YYYY-MM-dd) of the queried day.

userId.name: userId
userId.description: Unique identifier of the a user.

username.name: username
username.description: The username of the a user.

fileId.name: id
fileId.description: Unique identifier of the statistics file.

statisticsFileMonth.name: month
statisticsFileMonth.description: Date (format YYYY-MM-dd) specifying the month for which the statistics file will be downloaded (the day value is ignored).

systemOptionCategory.name: category
systemOptionCategory.description: The category of the system options.

systemOptionKey.name: key
systemOptionKey.description: The key of a system option.

fingerprint.name: fingerprint
fingerprint.description: Unique identifier of a trusted certificate.

loginOptionTypeOrId.name: typeOrId
loginOptionTypeOrId.description: The type or ID of the login option. The type's value is case insensitive and can be `OAUTH2`, `OAUTH2_INTERNAL` or `BASIC`.

featureKey.name: featureKey
featureKey.description: A unique key of the feature toggle.

queryParam_currentPage.name: currentPage
queryParam_currentPage.description: The current page of the paginated results.

queryParam_fragmentType.name: fragmentType
queryParam_fragmentType.description: A characteristic which identifies a managed object or event, for example, geolocation, electricity sensor, relay state.

queryParam_fragmentValue.name: fragmentValue
queryParam_fragmentValue.description:
Allows filtering events by the fragment's value, but only when provided together with `fragmentType`.

> **⚠️ Important:** Only fragments with a string value are supported.

queryParam_pageSize.name: pageSize
queryParam_pageSize.description: Indicates how many entries of the collection shall be returned. The upper limit for one page is 2,000 objects.

queryParam_skipChildrenNames.name: skipChildrenNames
queryParam_skipChildrenNames.description: When set to `true`, the returned references of child devices won't contain their names.

queryParam_query.name: query
queryParam_query.description: Use query language to perform operations and/or filter the results. Details about the properties and supported operations can be found in [Query language](#tag/Query-language).

queryParam_withParents.name: withParents
queryParam_withParents.description:
When withParents is set to `true`, the request will include all ancestors from all the levels above the device in the hierarchy. If no parents exist, an empty array will be returned.
> **ⓘ Info:** Inventory roles are not taken into consideration when collecting managed object parents, so basic information about all ancestors will be returned.

queryParam_withTotalPages.name: withTotalPages
queryParam_withTotalPages.description:
When set to `true`, the returned result will contain in the statistics object the total number of pages. Only applicable on [range queries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Range_query_(database)).
> **ⓘ Info:** To improve performance, the `totalPages` statistics are cached for 10 seconds.

queryParam_withTotalElements.name: withTotalElements
queryParam_withTotalElements.description:
When set to `true`, the returned result will contain in the statistics object the total number of elements. Only applicable on [range queries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Range_query_(database)).
> **ⓘ Info:** To improve performance, the `totalElements` statistics are cached for 10 seconds.

queryParam_hasVersions.name: hasVersions
queryParam_hasVersions.description: When set to `true`, the returned result contains applications with an `applicationVersions` field that is not empty. When set to `false`, the result will contain applications with an empty `applicationVersions` field.

queryParam_inventory_ids.name: ids
queryParam_inventory_ids.description:
The managed object IDs to search for.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you query for multiple IDs at once, comma-separate the values.

queryParam_inventory_childAssetId.name: childAssetId
queryParam_inventory_childAssetId.description: Search for a specific child asset and list all the groups to which it belongs.

queryParam_inventory_childDeviceId.name: childDeviceId
queryParam_inventory_childDeviceId.description: Search for a specific child device and list all the groups to which it belongs.

queryParam_inventory_childAdditionId.name: childAdditionId
queryParam_inventory_childAdditionId.description: Search for a specific child addition and list all the groups to which it belongs.

queryParam_inventory_type.name: type
queryParam_inventory_type.description: The type of managed object to search for.

queryParam_inventory_owner.name: owner
queryParam_inventory_owner.description: Username of the owner of the managed objects.

queryParam_inventory_textPrefix.name: text
queryParam_inventory_textPrefix.description:
Search for managed objects where a property value is equal to the given one.
The following properties are examined: `id, type, name, owner, externalIds`.

queryParam_inventory_withGroups.name: withGroups
queryParam_inventory_withGroups.description: When set to `true` it returns additional information about the groups to which the searched managed object belongs. This results in setting the `assetParents` property with additional information about the groups.

queryParam_inventory_withChildren.name: withChildren
queryParam_inventory_withChildren.description: Determines if children with ID and name should be returned when fetching the managed object. Set it to `false` to improve query performance. The default behavior can be controlled by the feature toggle `core.inventory.without.children`. When this toggle is disabled, the default value reverts to `true` for backward compatibility.

queryParam_inventory_withChildrenCount.name: withChildrenCount
queryParam_inventory_withChildrenCount.description: When set to `true`, the returned result will contain the total number of children in the respective objects (`childAdditions`, `childAssets` and `childDevices`).

queryParam_inventory_deviceQuery.name: q
queryParam_inventory_deviceQuery.description: Similar to the parameter `query`, but it assumes that this is a device query request and it adds automatically the search criteria `fragmentType=c8y_IsDevice`.

queryParam_inventory_onlyRoots.name: onlyRoots
queryParam_inventory_onlyRoots.description: When set to `true` it returns managed objects which don't have any parent. If the current user doesn't have access to the parent, this is also root for the user.

queryParam_inventory_cascade.name: cascade
queryParam_inventory_cascade.description: When set to `true` and the managed object is a device or group, all the hierarchy will be deleted.

queryParam_inventory_forceCascade.name: forceCascade
queryParam_inventory_forceCascade.description: When set to `true` all the hierarchy will be deleted without checking the type of managed object. It takes precedence over the parameter `cascade`.

queryParam_inventory_withDeviceUser.name: withDeviceUser
queryParam_inventory_withDeviceUser.description: When set to `true` and the managed object is a device, it deletes the associated device user (credentials).

queryParam_alarm_type.name: type
queryParam_alarm_type.description:
The types of alarm to search for.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you query for multiple alarm types at once, comma-separate the values. Space characters in alarm types must be escaped.

queryParam_alarm_source.name: source
queryParam_alarm_source.description: The managed object ID to which the alarm is associated.

queryParam_alarm_resolved.name: resolved
queryParam_alarm_resolved.description: When set to `true` only alarms with status CLEARED will be fetched, whereas `false` will fetch all alarms with status ACTIVE or ACKNOWLEDGED. Takes precedence over the `status` parameter.

queryParam_alarm_withSourceChildren.name: withSourceChildren
queryParam_alarm_withSourceChildren.description: When set to `true`, alarms for related source assets, devices and additions will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_alarm_withSourceAssets.name: withSourceAssets
queryParam_alarm_withSourceAssets.description: When set to `true`, alarms for related source assets will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_alarm_withSourceDevices.name: withSourceDevices
queryParam_alarm_withSourceDevices.description: When set to `true`, alarms for related source devices will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_alarm_withSourceAdditions.name: withSourceAdditions
queryParam_alarm_withSourceAdditions.description: When set to `true`, alarms for related source additions will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_alarm_severity.name: severity
queryParam_alarm_severity.description:
The severity of the alarm to search for.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you query for multiple alarm severities at once, comma-separate the values.

queryParam_alarm_status.name: status
queryParam_alarm_status.description:
The status of the alarm to search for. Should not be used when `resolved` parameter is provided.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you query for multiple alarm statuses at once, comma-separate the values.

queryParam_alarm_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_alarm_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the alarm occurrence.

queryParam_alarm_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_alarm_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the alarm occurrence.

queryParam_alarm_createdFrom.name: createdFrom
queryParam_alarm_createdFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the alarm creation.

queryParam_alarm_createdTo.name: createdTo
queryParam_alarm_createdTo.description: End date or date and time of the alarm creation.

queryParam_application_type.name: type
queryParam_application_type.description: The type of the application. It is possible to use multiple values separated by a comma. For example, `EXTERNAL,HOSTED` will return only applications with type `EXTERNAL` or `HOSTED`.

queryParam_application_owner.name: owner
queryParam_application_owner.description: The ID of the tenant that owns the applications.

queryParam_application_tenant.name: tenant
queryParam_application_tenant.description: The ID of a tenant that either owns the application or is subscribed to the applications.

queryParam_application_user.name: user
queryParam_application_user.description: The ID of a user that has access to the applications.

queryParam_application_subscriber.name: subscriber
queryParam_application_subscriber.description: The ID of a tenant that is subscribed to the applications.

queryParam_application_providedFor.name: providedFor
queryParam_application_providedFor.description: The ID of a tenant that is subscribed to the applications but doesn't own them.

queryParam_application_force.name: force
queryParam_application_force.description: Force deletion by unsubscribing all tenants from the application first and then deleting the application itself.

queryParam_application_version_version.name: version
queryParam_application_version_version.description: The version field of the application version.

queryParam_application_version_tag.name: tag
queryParam_application_version_tag.description: The tag of the application version.

queryParam_audit_application.name: application
queryParam_audit_application.description: Name of the application from which the audit was carried out.

queryParam_audit_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_audit_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the audit record (device time).

queryParam_audit_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_audit_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the audit record (device time).

queryParam_audit_source.name: source
queryParam_audit_source.description: The platform component ID to which the audit is associated.

queryParam_audit_type.name: type
queryParam_audit_type.description: The type of audit record to search for.

queryParam_event_type.name: type
queryParam_event_type.description: The type of event to search for.

queryParam_event_source.name: source
queryParam_event_source.description: The managed object ID to which the event is associated.

queryParam_event_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_event_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the event occurrence (provided by the device).

queryParam_event_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_event_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the event occurrence (provided by the device).

queryParam_event_withSourceChildren.name: withSourceChildren
queryParam_event_withSourceChildren.description: When set to `true`, events for related source assets, devices and additions will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_event_withSourceAssets.name: withSourceAssets
queryParam_event_withSourceAssets.description: When set to `true`, events for related source assets will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_event_withSourceDevices.name: withSourceDevices
queryParam_event_withSourceDevices.description: When set to `true`, events for related source devices will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_event_withSourceAdditions.name: withSourceAdditions
queryParam_event_withSourceAdditions.description: When set to `true`, events for related source additions will also be included in the response. When this parameter is provided a `source` must be specified.

queryParam_event_createdFrom.name: createdFrom
queryParam_event_createdFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the event's creation (set by the platform during creation).

queryParam_event_createdTo.name: createdTo
queryParam_event_createdTo.description: End date or date and time of the event's creation (set by the platform during creation).

queryParam_event_revert.name: revert
queryParam_event_revert.description:
If you are using a range query (that is, at least one of the `dateFrom` or `dateTo` parameters is included in the request), then setting `revert=true` will sort the results by the oldest events first.
By default, the results are sorted by the newest events first.

queryParam_operation_revert.name: revert
queryParam_operation_revert.description:
If you are using a range query (that is, at least one of the `dateFrom` or `dateTo` parameters is included in the request), then setting `revert=true` will sort the results by the newest operations first.
By default, the results are sorted by the oldest operations first.

queryParam_measurement_revert.name: revert
queryParam_measurement_revert.description:
If you are using a range query (that is, at least one of the `dateFrom` or `dateTo` parameters is included in the request), then setting `revert=false` will sort the 
results by the oldest measurements first and `revert=true` will sort the results by the newest measurements first. By default, the results are sorted by the newest 
measurements first for time series and by the oldest first for legacy measurements.

schema.default: true for time series and false for legacy measurements

queryParam_measurement_type.name: type
queryParam_measurement_type.description: The type of measurement to search for.

queryParam_measurement_source.name: source
queryParam_measurement_source.description: The managed object ID to which the measurement is associated.

queryParam_measurement_sourceRequired.name: source
queryParam_measurement_sourceRequired.description: The managed object ID to which the measurement is associated.

queryParam_measurement_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_measurement_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the measurement.

queryParam_measurement_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_measurement_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the measurement.

queryParam_measurement_dateFromRequired.name: dateFrom
queryParam_measurement_dateFromRequired.description: Start date or date and time of the measurement.

queryParam_measurement_dateToRequired.name: dateTo
queryParam_measurement_dateToRequired.description: End date or date and time of the measurement.

queryParam_measurement_valueFragmentType.name: valueFragmentType
queryParam_measurement_valueFragmentType.description: A characteristic which identifies the measurement.

queryParam_measurement_series.name: series
queryParam_measurement_series.description:
The specific series to search for.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you want to query multiple series at once, you must specify the parameter multiple times.

queryParam_measurement_valueFragmentSeri.name: valueFragmentSeries
queryParam_measurement_valueFragmentSeri.description: The specific series to search for.

queryParam_measurement_aggregationType.name: aggregationType
queryParam_measurement_aggregationType.description:
Fetch aggregated results as specified.
>**ⓘ Info:** When time series persistence is enabled, then this parameter is superseded by `aggregationInterval` and will be ignored if that parameter is specified. `aggregationType` should only be used for legacy persistence.

queryParam_measurement_aggregationInterv.name: aggregationInterval
queryParam_measurement_aggregationInterv.description:
Fetch results are aggregated using a time interval specified by an integer followed by a unit.
Available units of `s`econd, `m`inute, `h`our, `d`ay, `w`eek, `M`onth, `q`uarter and `y`ear are specified using their first letter.
Provided integer value must be positive with maximum three digits and without leading zeros.
>**ⓘ Info:** Full range of values for `aggregationInterval` parameter is only available when time series persistence is enabled. For legacy persistence the implementation will always fall back to `1d`, `1h` or `1m` based on the closest unit used.

queryParam_measurement_aggregationFuncti.name: aggregationFunction
queryParam_measurement_aggregationFuncti.description:
Selects aggregation functions that are calculated for each selected aggregation interval.
Providing this parameter requires also providing the `aggregationInterval` parameter.
By default `min` and `max` functions are calculated.
>**ⓘ Info:** This parameter is only available when time series persistence is enabled. For legacy persistence only `min` and `max` are calculated. If you want to calculate multiple functions at once, you must specify the parameter multiple times.

queryParam_notification_token.name: token
queryParam_notification_token.description: Subscriptions associated with this token will be removed.

queryParam_notification_subscription_sou.name: source
queryParam_notification_subscription_sou.description: The managed object ID to which the subscription is associated.

queryParam_notification_subscription_con.name: context
queryParam_notification_subscription_con.description: The context to which the subscription is associated.

queryParam_notification_subscription_sub.name: subscription
queryParam_notification_subscription_sub.description: The subscription name by which filtering will be done.

queryParam_notification_subscription_typ.name: typeFilter
queryParam_notification_subscription_typ.description:
The type used to filter subscriptions. This will check the subscription's `subscriptionFilter.typeFilter` field.
> **ⓘ Info:** Filtering by `typeFilter` may affect paging. Additional post filtering may be performed if OData-like expressions are used in the subscriptions.

queryParam_notification_subscription_con.name: context
queryParam_notification_subscription_con.description:
The context to which the subscription is associated.
> **ⓘ Info:** If the value is `mo` (managed object), then `source` must also be provided in the query.

queryParam_operation_agentId.name: agentId
queryParam_operation_agentId.description: An agent ID that may be part of the operation. If this parameter is set, the operation response objects contain the `deviceExternalIDs` object.

queryParam_operation_agentId_delete.name: agentId
queryParam_operation_agentId_delete.description: An agent ID that may be part of the operation.

queryParam_operation_deviceId.name: deviceId
queryParam_operation_deviceId.description: The ID of the device the operation is performed for.

queryParam_operation_fragmentType.name: fragmentType
queryParam_operation_fragmentType.description: The type of fragment that must be part of the operation.

queryParam_operation_bulkOperationId.name: bulkOperationId
queryParam_operation_bulkOperationId.description: The bulk operation ID that this operation belongs to.

queryParam_operation_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_operation_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the operation.

queryParam_operation_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_operation_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the operation.

queryParam_users_groups.name: groups
queryParam_users_groups.description:
Numeric group identifiers. The response will contain only users which belong to at least one of the specified groups.
>**ⓘ Info:** If you query for multiple user groups at once, comma-separate the values.

queryParam_users_owner.name: owner
queryParam_users_owner.description: Exact username of the owner of the user

queryParam_users_onlyDevices.name: onlyDevices
queryParam_users_onlyDevices.description:
If set to `true`, the response will only contain users created during bootstrap process (starting with “device_”).
If the flag is absent or `false` the result will not contain “device_” users.

queryParam_users_withSubusersCount.name: withSubusersCount
queryParam_users_withSubusersCount.description:
If set to `true`, then each of returned user will contain an additional field “subusersCount”.
It is the number of direct subusers (users with corresponding “owner”).

queryParam_tenant_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_tenant_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the statistics.

queryParam_tenant_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_tenant_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the statistics.

queryParam_tenant_tenant.name: tenant
queryParam_tenant_tenant.description: Unique identifier of a Cumulocity tenant.

queryParam_tenantId.name: tenantId
queryParam_tenantId.description: Unique identifier of a Cumulocity tenant.

queryParam_tenant_id.name: tenant_id
queryParam_tenant_id.description: Unique identifier of a Cumulocity tenant. If not provided, the tenant is calculated based on the request domain.

queryParam_tenant_withParent.name: withParent
queryParam_tenant_withParent.description: When set to `true`, the returned result will contain the parent of the current tenant.

queryParam_tenant_company.name: company
queryParam_tenant_company.description: Company name associated with the Cumulocity tenant.

queryParam_tenant_domain.name: domain
queryParam_tenant_domain.description: Domain name of the Cumulocity tenant.

queryParam_tenant_parent.name: parent
queryParam_tenant_parent.description: Identifier of the Cumulocity tenant's parent. Works only for requests sent with management tenant credentials.

queryParam_deviceId.name: deviceId
queryParam_deviceId.description: The ID of the device to search for.

queryParam_file_dateFrom.name: dateFrom
queryParam_file_dateFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the range included in statistics file.

queryParam_file_dateTo.name: dateTo
queryParam_file_dateTo.description: End date or date and time of the range included in statistics file.

queryParam_lastUpdatedFrom.name: lastUpdatedFrom
queryParam_lastUpdatedFrom.description: Start date or date and time of the last update made.

queryParam_lastUpdatedTo.name: lastUpdatedTo
queryParam_lastUpdatedTo.description: End date or date and time of the last update made.

queryParam_tenant_management.name: management
queryParam_tenant_management.description:
If this is set to `true`, the management tenant login options will be returned.

> **ⓘ Info:** The `tenantId` parameter must not be present in the request when using the `management` parameter, otherwise it will cause an error.

queryParam_targetTenant.name: targetTenant
queryParam_targetTenant.description: Unique identifier of a Cumulocity tenant.

queryParam_addToTrustStore.name: addToTrustStore
queryParam_addToTrustStore.description:
If set to `true` the certificate is added to the truststore.

The truststore contains all trusted certificates. A connection to a device is only established if it connects to Cumulocity with a certificate in the truststore.

queryParam_withLatestValues.name: withLatestValues
queryParam_withLatestValues.description:
If set to true the platform returns managed objects with the fragment `c8y_LatestMeasurements`, which contains the latest measurement values reported by the device to the platform. Additionally returned managed objects can have fragment `c8y_PreviousMeasurements` if there was a value of a given series previous to the latest.  

> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The parameter is a part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public preview.

queryParam_terminateUserSessions.name: terminateUserSessions
queryParam_terminateUserSessions.description: When set to 'true', all active sessions for all users within the tenant will be terminated.

queryParam_forceLogout.name: forceLogout
queryParam_forceLogout.description: If set to `true`, users with this global role will be force logged out.

sslCertChainInHeader.name: X-Ssl-Cert-Chain
sslCertChainInHeader.description: Used to send a certificate chain in the header. Separate the chain with ` ` (a space character) and also each 64 bit block with ` ` (a space character).

count.description: Number of times that this alarm has been triggered.

creationTime.description: The date and time when the alarm was created.

firstOccurrenceTime.description: The time at which the alarm occurred for the first time. Only present when `count` is greater than 1.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the alarm was last updated.

source.description: The managed object to which the alarm is associated.

status.description: The status of the alarm. If not specified, a new alarm will be created as ACTIVE.

time.description: The date and time when the alarm is triggered.

type.description: Identifies the type of this alarm.

additionalProperties.description:
It is possible to add an arbitrary number of additional properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property1": {}`, `"property2": "value"`. These properties are known as custom fragments and can be of any type, for example, object or string. Each custom fragment is identified by a unique name.

Review [Getting started > Technical concepts > Cumulocity's domain model > Inventory > Fragments > Naming conventions of fragments](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/concepts/domain-model/#naming-conventions-of-fragments) in the Cumulocity user documentation as there are characters that can not be used when naming custom fragments.

auditRecordsForApplication.description: Read-only collection of audit records for a specific application. The placeholder {application} must be the name of a registered application.

auditRecordsForType.description: Read-only collection of audit records for a specific type.

auditRecordsForUser.description: Read-only collection of audit records for a specific user. The placeholder {user} must be a username of a registered user.

auditRecordsForTypeAndApplication.description: Read-only collection of audit records for specific type and application.

auditRecordsForUserAndApplication.description: Read-only collection of audit records for specific user and application.

auditRecordsForUserAndType.description: Read-only collection of audit records for specific user and type.

activity.description: Summary of the action that was carried out.

application.description: Name of the application that performed the action.

action.description: The action that was carried out.

changes.description: Collection of objects describing the changes that were carried out.

changeType.description: The type of change that was carried out.

newValue.description: The new value of the object.

cpu.description: Number of CPUs or CPU time limit.

previousValue.description: The previous value of the object.

cpu.description: Number of CPUs or CPU time limit.

type.description: The type of the object.

id.description: Unique identifier of the audit record.

severity.description: The severity of the audit action.

source.description: The platform component ID to which the audit is associated.

text.description: Details of the action that was carried out.

time.description: The date and time when the audit is updated.

type.description: Identifies the platform component of the audit.

user.description: The user who carried out the activity.

additionalProperties.description: It is possible to add an arbitrary number of additional properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property1": {}`, `"property2": "value"`. These properties can be of any type, for example, object or string.

auditRecords.description: An array containing the results of the request.

alarmsForSource.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a specific source object. The placeholder {source} must be a unique ID of an object in the inventory.

alarmsForStatus.description: Read-only collection of all alarms in a particular status. The placeholder {status} can be one of the following values: ACTIVE, ACKNOWLEDGED or CLEARED

alarmsForSourceAndStatusAndTime.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a specific source, status and time range.

alarmsForStatusAndTime.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a particular status and time range.

alarmsForSourceAndTime.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a specific source and time range.

alarmsForTime.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a particular time range.

alarmsForSourceAndStatus.description: Read-only collection of all alarms for a specific source object in a particular status.

availability.description: Application access level for other tenants.

contextPath.description: The context path in the URL makes the application accessible. Mandatory when the type of the application is `HOSTED`.

id.description: Unique identifier of the application.

key.description: Applications, regardless of their form, are identified by an application key.

type.description: The type of the application.

breadcrumbs.description:
A flag to indicate if the application has a breadcrumbs navigation on the UI.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

contentSecurityPolicy.description:
The content security policy of the application.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

dynamicOptionsUrl.description:
A URL to a JSON object with dynamic content options.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

globalTitle.description:
The global title of the application.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

legacy.description:
A flag that shows if the application is a legacy application or not.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

rightDrawer.description:
A flag to indicate if the application uses the UI context menu on the right side.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

upgrade.description:
A flag that shows if the application is hybrid and using Angular and AngularJS simultaneously.
> **ⓘ Info:** This property is specific to the web application type.

activeVersionId.description: The active version ID of the application. For microservice applications the active version ID is the microservice manifest version ID.

resourcesUrl.description: URL to the application base directory hosted on an external server. Only present in legacy hosted applications.

application_owner.description: Reference to the tenant owning this application. The default value is a reference to the current tenant.

id.description: The tenant ID.

application_required_roles.description: List of permissions required by a microservice to work.

microservice_application_manifest.description: The manifest of the microservice application.

apiVersion.description: Document type format discriminator, for future changes in format.

billingMode.description:
The billing mode of the application.

In case of RESOURCES, the number of resources used is exposed for billing calculation per usage.
In case of SUBSCRIPTION, all resources usage is counted for the microservice owner and the subtenant is charged for subscription.

contextPath.description: The context path in the URL makes the application accessible.

extensions.description: A list of URL extensions for this microservice application.

path.description: The relative path in Cumulocity for this microservice application.

type.description: The type of this extension.

isolation.description:
Deployment isolation.
In case of PER_TENANT, there is a separate instance for each tenant.
Otherwise, there is one single instance for all subscribed tenants.
This will affect billing.

allOf.description: Defines the strategy used to verify if a microservice is alive or requires a restart.

provider.description:
Application provider information.
Simple name allowed for predefined providers, for example, c8y.
Detailed object for external provider.

name.description: The name of the application provider.

allOf.description: Defines the strategy used to verify if a microservice is ready to accept traffic.

requestResources.description: The minimum required resources for the microservice application.

cpu.description: The required CPU resource for this microservice application.

memory.description: The required memory resource for this microservice application.

resources.description: The recommended resources for this microservice application.

cpu.description: The required CPU resource for this microservice application.

memory.description: The required memory resource for this microservice application.

scale.description:
Allows to configure a microservice auto scaling policy.
If the microservice uses a lot of CPU resources, a second instance will be created automatically when this is set to `AUTO`.
The default is `NONE`, meaning auto scaling will not happen.

settingsCategory.description:
Allows to specify a custom category for microservice settings.
By default, `contextPath` is used.

version.description:
Application version.
Must be a correct [SemVer](https://semver.org/) value but the "+" sign is disallowed.

web_application_manifest.description: The manifest of the web application.

_webpaas.description:
A legacy flag that identified a certain type of web application that would control the behavior of plugin tab in the application details view.
It is no longer used.

contentSecurityPolicy.description: The content security policy of the application.
contentSecurityPolicy.example: base-uri 'none'; default-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' http: https: ws: wss:; connect-src 'self' *.billwerk.com http: https: ws: wss:;  script-src 'self' open.mapquestapi.com *.twitter.com *.twimg.com 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' data:; style-src * 'unsafe-inline' blob:; img-src * data:; font-src * data:; frame-src *;

noAppSwitcher.description: A flag that decides if the application is shown in the app switcher on the UI.

tabsHorizontal.description: A flag that decides if the application tabs are displayed horizontally or not.

initialDelaySeconds.description: The probe's initial delay in seconds.

httpGet.description: The probe's HTTP GET method information.

application_settings.description: A list of settings objects for this microservice application.

valueSchema.description: The value schema determines the values that the microservice can process.

type.description: The value schema type.

editable.description: Indicates if the value is editable.

inheritFromOwner.description: Indicated wether this setting is inherited.

numberOfAll.description: Number of lines processed from the CSV file, without the first line (column headers).

numberOfFailed.description: Number of failed creations of device credentials.

numberOfSuccessful.description: Number of successful creations of device credentials. This counts both create and update operations.

credentialUpdatedList.description: An array with the updated device credentials.

failedCreationList.description: An array with details of the failed device credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier of this bulk operation.

groupId.description:
Identifies the target group on which this operation should be performed.
>**ⓘ Info:** `groupId` and `failedParentId` are mutually exclusive. Use only one of them in your request.

failedParentId.description:
Identifies the failed bulk operation from which the failed operations should be rescheduled.
>**ⓘ Info:** `groupId` and `failedParentId` are mutually exclusive. Use only one of them in your request.

startDate.description: Date and time when the operations of this bulk operation should be created.

creationRamp.description: Delay between every operation creation in seconds.

operationPrototype.description: Operation to be executed for every device in a group.

status.description: The status of this bulk operation, in context of the execution of all its single operations.

generalStatus.description: The general status of this bulk operation. The general status is visible for end users and they can filter and evaluate bulk operations by this status.

progress.description: Contains information about the number of processed operations.

id.description: A unique identifier for this user.

lastPasswordChange.description: The date and time when the user's password was last changed, in [ISO 8601 datetime format](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

password.description: The user's password. Only Latin1 characters are allowed.

shouldResetPassword.description: Indicates if the user should reset the password on the next login.

twoFactorAuthenticationEnabled.description: Indicates if the user has to use two-factor authentication to log in.

userName.description: The user's username. It can have a maximum of 1000 characters.

customProperties.description: An object with a list of custom properties.

language.description: The preferred language to be used in the platform.

additionalProperties.description: It is possible to add an arbitrary number of custom properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property": "value"`.

operations.description: An array containing the registered operations.

operationsByStatus.description: Read-only collection of all operations with a particular status.

operationsByAgentId.description: Read-only collection of all operations targeting a particular agent.

operationsByAgentIdAndStatus.description: Read-only collection of all operations targeting a particular agent and with a particular status.

operationsByDeviceId.description: Read-only collection of all operations to be executed on a particular device.

operationsByDeviceIdAndStatus.description: Read-only collection of all operations with a particular status, that should be executed on a particular device.

id.description: The external ID of the device.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for these device credentials.

securityToken.description:
Security token which is required and verified against during device request acceptance.
See [Security token policy](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/device-management-application/registering-devices/#security-token-policy) for more details on configuration.
See [Update specific new device request status](#operation/putNewDeviceRequestResource) for details on submitting token upon device acceptance.

eventsForSource.description: Read-only collection of all events for a specific source object. The placeholder {source} must be a unique ID of an object in the inventory.

eventsForSourceAndType.description: Read-only collection of all events of a particular type and a specific source object.

eventsForType.description: Read-only collection of all events of a particular type.

eventsForFragmentType.description: Read-only collection of all events containing a particular fragment type.

eventsForTime.description: Read-only collection of all events for a particular time range.

eventsForSourceAndTime.description: Read-only collection of all events for a specific source object in a particular time range.

externalId.description: The identifier used in the external system that Cumulocity interfaces with.

managedObject.description: The managed object linked to the external ID.

type.description: The type of the external identifier.

externalIds.description: An array containing the details of all external IDs (if any).

creationTime.description: The date and time when the event was created.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the event was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier of the event.

source.description: The managed object to which the event is associated.

time.description: The date and time when the event is updated.

type.description: Identifies the type of this event.

It is possible to add an arbitrary number of additional properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property1": {}`, `"property2": "value"`. These properties are known as custom fragments and can be of any type, for example, object or string. Each custom fragment is identified by a unique name.

Review [Getting started > Technical concepts > Cumulocity's domain model > Inventory > Fragments > Naming conventions of fragments](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/concepts/domain-model/#naming-conventions-of-fragments) in the Cumulocity user documentation as there are characters that can not be used when naming custom fragments.

key.description: A unique key of the feature toggle.

phase.description: Current phase of feature toggle rollout.

active.description: Current value of the feature toggle marking whether the feature is active or not.

strategy.description: The source of the feature toggle value - either it's feature toggle definition provided default, or per tenant provided override.

active.description: Current value of the feature toggle marking whether the feature is active or not.

tenantId.description: Identifier of a tenant this feature toggle value is for.

active.description: Current value of the feature toggle marking whether the feature is active or not.

id.description: A unique identifier for this group.

roles.description: An object containing user roles for this group.

references.description: A list of user role references.

users.description: The list of users in this group.

references.description: The list of users in this group.

externalId.description: Single external ID, represented by the type and the value of the external ID.

externalIdsOfGlobalId.description: Represents a collection of external IDs for a specified global ID.

managedObjectsForFragmentType.description: Read-only collection of all managed objects with a particular fragment type or capability (placeholder {fragmentType}).

managedObjectsForType.description: Read-only collection of all managed objects of a particular type (placeholder {type}).

references.description: An array containing the referenced managed objects.

type.description: Media type of the file.

name.description: Name of the attachment. If it is not provided in the request, it will be set as the event ID.

type.description: Media type of the attachment.

roles.description: An array of roles that are assigned to the managed object for the user.

A unique identifier for this inventory assignment.

managedObject.description: A unique identifier for the managed object for which the roles are assigned.

roles.description: An array of roles that are assigned to the managed object for the user.

self.description: A URL linking to this resource.

description.description: A description for this inventory role.

id.description: A unique identifier for this inventory role.

name.description: The name of this inventory role.

permissions.description: A set of permissions for this inventory role.

lastUpdated.description: Date and time of the file's last update.

length.description: Size of the file in bytes.

name.description: Name of the managed object. It is set from the `object` contained in the payload.

owner.description: Username of the owner of the file.

type.description: Type of the managed object. It is set from the `object` contained in the payload.

creationTime.description: The date and time when the object was created.

id.description: Unique identifier of the object.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the object was updated for the last time.

type.description: The fragment type can be interpreted as _device class_, this means, devices with the same type can receive the same types of configuration, software, firmware and operations. The type value is indexed and is therefore used for queries.

c8y_IsDevice.description: A fragment which identifies this managed object as a device.

c8y_IsDeviceGroup.description: A fragment which identifies this managed object as a device group.

c8y_DeviceTypes.description: This fragment must be added in order to publish sample commands for a subset of devices sharing the same device type. If the fragment is present, the list of sample commands for a device type will be extended with the sample commands for the `c8y_DeviceTypes`. New sample commands created from the user interface will be created in the context of the `c8y_DeviceTypes`.

c8y_SupportedOperations.description: Lists the operations that are available for a particular device, so that applications can trigger the operations.

additionalProperties.description:
It is possible to add an arbitrary number of additional properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property1": {}`, `"property2": "value"`. These properties are known as custom fragments and can be of any type, for example, object or string. Each custom fragment is identified by a unique name.

Review [Getting started > Technical concepts > Cumulocity's domain model > Inventory > Fragments > Naming conventions of fragments](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/concepts/domain-model/#naming-conventions-of-fragments) in the Cumulocity user documentation as there are characters that can not be used when naming custom fragments.

externalId.description: The identifier used in the external system that Cumulocity interfaces with.

lastMessage.description: The time when the device sent the last message to Cumulocity.

LatestMeasurementFragment.description:
The read only fragment which contains the latest measurements series reported by the device.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

LatestMeasurementValue.description:
The read only fragment which contains the latest measurements series values reported by the device.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

unit.description: The unit of the measurement series.

time.description: The time of the measurement series.

value.description: The value of the individual measurement.

c8y_LatestMeasurements.description:
The read only fragment which contains the latest measurements reported by the device.
The returned optionally only if the query parameter `withLatestValues=true` is used.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

PreviousMeasurementFragment.description:
The read only fragment which contains the previous to latest measurements series reported by the device.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

PreviousMeasurementValue.description:
The read only fragment which contains the previous to latest measurements series values reported by the device.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

unit.description: The unit of the measurement series.

time.description: The time of the measurement series.

value.description: The value of the individual measurement.

c8y_PreviousMeasurements.description:
The read only fragment which contains the previous to latest measurements reported by the device.
The returned optionally only if the query parameter `withLatestValues=true` is used.
> **⚠️ Feature Preview:** The feature is part of the Latest Measurement feature which is still under public feature preview.

additionalProperties.description:
It is possible to add an arbitrary number of additional properties as a list of key-value pairs, for example, `"property1": {}`, `"property2": "value"`. These properties are known as custom fragments and can be of any type, for example, object or string. Each custom fragment is identified by a unique name.

Review [Getting started > Technical concepts > Cumulocity's domain model > Inventory > Fragments > Naming conventions of fragments](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/concepts/domain-model/#naming-conventions-of-fragments) in the Cumulocity user documentation as there are characters that can not be used when naming custom fragments.

securityToken.description:
When accepting a device request, the security token is verified against the token submitted by the device when requesting credentials.
See [Security token policy](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/device-management-application/registering-devices/#security-token-policy) for details on configuration.
See [Create device credentials](#operation/postDeviceCredentialsCollectionResource) for details on creating token for device registration.
`securityToken` parameter can be added only when submitting `ACCEPTED` status.

Review [Getting started > Technical concepts > Cumulocity's domain model > Inventory > Fragments > Naming conventions of fragments](https://www.cumulocity.com/docs/concepts/domain-model/#naming-conventions-of-fragments) in the Cumulocity user documentation as there are characters that can not be used when naming custom fragments.

pageSize.description: Indicates the number of objects that the collection may contain per page. The upper limit for one page is 2,000 objects.

totalPages.description:
The total number of paginated results (pages).

> **ⓘ Info:** This property is returned by default except when an operation retrieves all records where values are between an upper and lower boundary, for example, querying ranges using `dateFrom`–`dateTo`. In such cases, the query parameter `withTotalPages=true` should be used to include the total number of pages (at the expense of slightly slower performance).

currentUserPassword.description: The current password of the user performing the request.

newPassword.description:
The new password to be set for the user performing the request.

By default, the password must meet the following conditions:

* Consist of at least eight characters (this parameter can be configured by the management tenant.)
* It must not have been used previously by user.
* Include each of the following character types:
  * uppercase letters: `[A-Z]`, for example `ABCDEF`.
  * lowercase letters: `[a-z]`, for example `abcdef`.
  * numbers: `[0-9]`, for example: `123456`.
  * any other symbol from following list `` `~!@#$%^&*()_|+-=?;:'",.<>{}[]\/`` as a special character, for example `!@#$%^`.

> **ⓘ Info:** The password rules can be configured by the administrator, that means, your administrator can configure your account to enforce a password policy. You may be required to pick a strong password for example or to change your password regularly.

fragmentType.description: The fragment type(s) to which the rule is applied. Used by the data types EVENT, MEASUREMENT, OPERATION and BULK_OPERATION.

lastPasswordChange.description: The date and time when the user's password was last changed, in [ISO 8601 datetime format](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

owner.description: Identifier of the parent user. If present, indicates that a user belongs to a user hierarchy by pointing to its direct ancestor. Can only be set by users with role USER_MANAGEMENT_ADMIN during user creation. Otherwise it's assigned automatically.

password.description:
The user's password. Only Latin1 characters are allowed.

If you do not specify a password when creating a new user with a POST request, it must contain the property `sendPasswordResetEmail` with a value of `true`.

passwordStrength.description: Indicates the password strength. The value can be GREEN, YELLOW or RED for decreasing password strengths.

roles.description: An object with a list of user roles.

references.description: A list of user role references.

sendPasswordResetEmail.description:
When set to `true`, this field will cause Cumulocity to send a password reset email to the email address specified.

If there is no password specified when creating a new user with a POST request, this must be specified and it must be set to `true`.

shouldResetPassword.description: Indicates if the user should reset the password on the next login.

twoFactorAuthenticationEnabled.description: Indicates if the user has to use two-factor authentication to log in.

userName.description: The user's username. It cannot contain whitespace, slashes nor any of `+$:` characters.
